_comment: pecli ligandmpnn config for binder-block sequence design. NA-aware inverse folding on the relaxed rfd3na complex. The DNA MUST remain in the input PDB (it is the bound context ligandmpnn conditions on). Field names follow pecli/tools/guides/ligandmpnn.md. Paper: temperature 0.1, 5 sequences per backbone.
_chains_note: chains_to_design = the designed PROTEIN chain only (the DNA chains are held as context, never designed). The exact chain id depends on how rfd3na labels the designed chain in its output PDB -- set it from the actual output (typically the chain after the DNA chains). Do NOT list the DNA chains here.
_fixed_residues_note: Unlike enzyme design, there are no catalytic residues to hold fixed here -- the whole protein interface is designed. Leave fixed_residues empty unless a specific motif is being grafted.

packing._comment: Write full-atom PDBs (packed/ subfolder) so the downstream fold + H-bond interaction counting sees real side-chain atoms against the DNA.

_ranking_note: Rank designs by higher overall_confidence; weight ligand_confidence (positions near the DNA) for interface quality. First FASTA record is the WT input; subsequent records are the designs.
